questions.questionText: In Germany, people are allowed to openly say something against the government because...

questions.questionText: In Germany, parents can decide until their child's 14th birthday whether they participate in...
Germany is a constitutional state. What does this mean?
options: All residents and the state must follow the laws.
The state does not have to follow the laws.
Only Germans must follow the laws.
questions.correctAnswer: All residents and the state must follow the laws.

questions.questionText: Which right belongs to the basic rights in Germany?

Only persons who have never been in prison are allowed to vote.
The voter may neither be influenced nor forced to vote in a certain way and may not suffer disadvantages from voting.
questions.correctAnswer: The voter may neither be influenced nor forced to vote in a certain way and may not suffer disadvantages from voting.

questions.questionText: What is the German constitution called?

Every person may express their opinion.
All are equal before the law.
questions.correctAnswer: Everyone should have the same amount of money.

questions.questionText: Which basic right in Germany applies only to foreigners? The basic right to...

Yes, but two-thirds of the members in the Bundestag must be in favor.
No, because freedom of the press is a basic right. It cannot be abolished.
No, because only the Bundesrat can abolish freedom of the press.
questions.correctAnswer: No, because freedom of the press is a basic right. It cannot be abolished.

questions.questionText: In parliament, the term 'opposition' stands for...
